signals.headline: Operation Epic Fury Ceasefire Confirmed | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Operation Epic Fury Ceasefire Confirmed | YES | HIGH confidence**

Iran has agreed to a **ceasefire** and agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz following Operation Epic Fury, a 38-day military campaign that concluded in early April 2026[2]. The Trump Administration is now negotiating a broader peace agreement[2].

signals.headline: Military Objectives Achieved | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Military Objectives Achieved | YES | HIGH confidence**

The U.S. military completed its stated objectives within 38 days, including destroying Iran's **ballistic missile and drone capabilities**, **navy**, and **defense industrial base**[2]. According to Chairman of the Joint Chiefs General Dan Caine, all three distinct military objectives defined by the President were achieved[2].

signals.headline: Iran's Nuclear Program Severely Degraded | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Iran's Nuclear Program Severely Degraded | YES | HIGH confidence**

Earlier operations (Operation Midnight Hammer, referenced in June 2025) targeted Iran's nuclear sites, with officials claiming "obliteration" of nuclear facilities[1]. The broader Operation Epic Fury campaign further constrained Iran's ability to develop nuclear weapons as one of its core objectives[5].

signals.raw:
SOUTH KOREA INFLATION RISES TO 2.2% IN MARCH 2026, BELOW FORECASTS | NO | MED confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation rate increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% in prior months, missing market expectations of 2.4% and staying near the Bank of Korea's 2% target amid contained price pressures.[1] This recent data point, from Statistics Korea, could mildly shift prediction markets on region
